extra.reasoning:
The recurrence is acc := ((3*acc) + c) mod 64, where the nested if-then-else chain just implements mod 64 by subtracting 64 up to three times (max value 3*63+48=237, and 237-3*64=45<64). The step constants are c=48 (step_0), c=46 (step_1), c=44 (step_2) — all even. The faulty resume path t_resume_glitch fires only when opted_out and acc==61, setting sent:=true (the violation). For a violation we would need to drive acc from its initial value 0 to 61 (odd) and then issue a send_attempt while opted_out.

This is impossible: acc starts at 0 (even). Every step adds an even constant to 3*acc; 3*(even) is even, plus even is even, and mod 64 preserves parity. So acc is invariantly even on every reachable execution. The target 61 is odd and therefore unreachable. optout and send_attempt never modify acc.

I verified two ways. (1) BFS over acc in [0,63] from start 0 reaches exactly the 32 even values {0,2,...,62} and never 61. (2) Full state-space exploration over (opted_out, sent, acc) across all event interleavings yields 96 reachable states, none with opted_out=true and sent=true, and acc=61 is never reached. Hence t_resume_glitch can never fire; after optout, every send_attempt hits t_send_blocked (acc!=61 always holds) leaving sent=false. The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds. This is one of the unreachable-target cases, so no counterexample exists; arrays left empty.
